text.tspan: 16 four-bit values share one FP8 scale factor. That's microscaling.
Each cell: 4 bits = 1 sign + 2 exponent + 1 mantissa (E2M1 floating point)
Why FP8 not FP32: cheaper, ~no loss.
INT4 has no exponent. One outlier in a block ruins quantization for the whole block.
NVFP4's per-block scale handles outliers. Result: BF16 quality at one-quarter the memory.
The quantization deep dive has the format comparison and quality vs throughput tradeoff in detail.
